plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Reduce QQQ, SMH, and QQQI due to downtrends and to lower overall market exposure.
Add ASML, a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner setup, for diversification.
This plan aims to significantly de-risk the portfolio, focusing on stability.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer of 15-20% by reducing existing positions.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5% per month return, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leverage and concentration, but potential upside is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce overall leverage.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero, aligning with risk-first growth.
Reduce QQQ and SMH due to downtrends, reallocating capital to stronger positions.
Increase CRWD exposure, as it is in a strong uptrend and meets AI/Software allocation.
Add ASML, a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner setup, for diversification.
This plan aims for balanced growth while maintaining a ~10% cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still participating in quality tech, offering a moderate risk/reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, reallocating capital to higher-conviction plays.
Reduce SOXL to 5% and sell SPAL, optimizing leveraged ETF exposure to stronger setups.
Trim QQQ due to its downtrend, freeing capital for aggressive growth opportunities.
Increase CRWD exposure to 20%, leveraging its strong uptrend and high scanner score.
Add ASML at 10%, a high-quality tech name with a strong accumulation signal.
Buy SOXS at 5% to potentially hedge semiconductor exposure with a strong short setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4% per month, seeking stretch growth by leveraging high-conviction setups. Be aware that this mode significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay and rebalancing, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and rebalance towards income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing stability.
Sell QQQ and SMH to reduce growth-oriented exposure and high-beta assets.
Reduce CRWD to 5%, minimizing single-name high-beta exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to 30% for enhanced monthly distributions and income focus.
This plan aims to park cash for yield, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable income.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1% per month, focusing on steady monthly distributions. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in selloffs; this is income-focused, not a savings account.
